message:
Try this little experiment tonight: catch your dog's eye, let out a big, slow yawn… and watch what happens. 🐶🥱 There's a good chance they'll yawn right back at you.

It turns out yawns are contagious between species. In one well-known study, around 72% of dogs yawned after watching a human do it — an even higher "catch rate" than people have with each other. Your dog isn't bored or sleepy. They're tuning in to you.

And here's the heart-melting part: dogs are far more likely to catch a yawn from someone they LOVE. They'll mirror their own person's yawn much more readily than a stranger's, which leads many researchers to believe it's tied to your emotional bond — a quiet little echo of the connection you two share. (Scientists are still happily debating the finer details.)

So that sleepy yawn you both share on the couch at the end of a long day? It might be one of the most genuinely affectionate things your dog does — a tiny, involuntary "I'm right here with you." 🥰

Give it a go and report back: does YOUR dog catch your yawns? 🐾
